categories.name: React Development
categories.description: Building interactive user interfaces using React hooks and ecosystem tools.

topics.name: SWR Fundamentals
topics.description: Core concepts of the stale-while-revalidate fetching strategy.

topics.name: Data Fetching Patterns
topics.description: Strategies for remote data retrieval, caching, and synchronization.

topics.name: Advanced SWR Features
topics.description: Pagination, dependent fetching, and local mutations.

questions.question_text: What does the initialism 'SWR' stand for in the context of the Vercel React Hooks library?
questions.explanation: SWR stands for stale-while-revalidate, a strategy that first returns data from the cache (stale), then sends the fetch request (revalidate), and finally provides up-to-date data.
questions.hint: It describes a three-step caching strategy.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

questions.question_text: What is the primary advantage of using SWR over standard HTTP clients like Axios or the Fetch API?
questions.explanation: While Axios and Fetch handle requests and responses, SWR provides built-in features like caching, pagination, scroll position recovery, and dependent fetching out of the box.
questions.hint: Think about features that go beyond simple request/response handling.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

choices.text: SWR is faster at the initial TCP handshake.
SWR replaces the need for an API backend.
SWR provides automatic caching and revalidation features.
SWR is the only library that supports JSON transformation.

questions.question_text: Which component is used to configure SWR globally so that a 'fetcher' function can be shared across the entire application?
questions.explanation: SWRConfig is a provider component that allows child components to inherit global configuration, such as a common fetcher function.
questions.hint: It is a provider wrapper mentioned for 'Configuring SWR Globally'.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

questions.question_text: What performance issue does SWR's 'Dependent Fetching' feature help avoid when dealing with relational data?
questions.explanation: Dependent fetching allows SWR to fetch data that depends on another request while specifically avoiding 'waterfalls' (unnecessary sequential delays).
questions.hint: It is a common term for sequential network requests that slow down performance.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

questions.question_text: True or False: By default, SWR automatically revalidates (updates) data when a user re-focuses the page or switches back to the tab.
questions.explanation: Focus revalidation is a built-in feature of SWR that is enabled by default to keep data synchronized when the user returns to the page.
questions.hint: Check the default status of 'Focus Revalidation'.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

questions.question_text: The useSWRPages hook receives four specific arguments for pagination. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: According to the source, useSWRPages receives: 1. The key of the request, 2. A function to fetch the data, 3. A function that takes the SWR object for the next page, and 4. An array of dependencies.
questions.hint: One of these is usually associated with useEffect but is also used here.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

choices.text: A function to fetch the data

learning_cards.title: The 3 Steps of SWR
learning_cards.front: What are the three internal steps SWR handles for remote data fetching?
learning_cards.back: 1. Return data from cache (stale). 2. Send the fetch request (revalidate). 3. Return up-to-date data.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

learning_cards.title: Local Mutation & Offline-First
learning_cards.front: How does the 'Local Mutation' feature in SWR support an 'Offline-first' approach?
learning_cards.back: It allows setting a temporary local state that updates the UI immediately while waiting for the remote revalidation to finish.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching

learning_cards.title: Backend Agnostic Meaning
learning_cards.front: What does it mean that SWR is 'backend agnostic'?
learning_cards.back: It can fetch data from any kind of API or database (REST, GraphQL, etc.) as long as a fetcher function is provided.

source.title: An Introduction To SWR: React Hooks For Remote Data Fetching
